title: Slow-cooker vegetable curry
subtitle: A gentle, golden weekday curry, no frying first.
excerpt: Sweet potato, chickpeas and spinach slow-cooked in a tomato and coconut milk base with warming spices for six to eight hours. Fragrant and filling, no pre-frying, no standing over the hob. Serve with rice or flatbreads. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Anglo-Indian vegetable curry, slow-cooker adaptation. The spice combination draws on North Indian home-cooking traditions as documented in Madhur Jaffrey's Indian Cookery (1982).

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well for up to three months. Defrost overnight; stir in a splash of coconut milk when reheating.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles easily if your slow cooker is large enough. Excellent meal-prep dish.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the day before; the spices bloom overnight in the fridge and the flavour deepens.

content.text: Everything goes in raw. Sweet potato and chickpeas handle eight hours in a slow cooker without falling apart; spinach goes in right at the end, just before serving. The coconut milk softens the spices into something gentle and fragrant rather than hot. No frying the spices first, no standing over a pan. Put it on in the morning; come home to dinner.

Put {{sweet-potato}} cubed sweet potato, {{chickpeas-tinned}} of drained chickpeas, {{onion}} chopped onion, {{garlic}} of grated garlic, {{ginger-root}} of grated ginger, {{tinned-tomatoes}} of tinned tomatoes, {{coconut-milk}} of coconut milk, {{curry-powder}} of curry powder, {{garam-masala}} of garam masala, {{turmeric}} of turmeric and {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt into the slow cooker. Stir to combine. Cook on LOW for 6 to 8 hours or HIGH for 3 to 4 hours until the sweet potato is tender.
Five minutes before serving, stir {{spinach}} of spinach into the hot curry. Put the lid back on for 5 minutes until wilted. Taste and adjust salt.

items.symptom: Sweet potato went mushy and dissolved
items.cause: Overcooked, or pieces cut too small.
items.fix: Cut to 3 cm and check at 6 hours on LOW; some slow cookers run hot. A slightly broken-down sweet potato is normal and good; total dissolution means it went too long.

items.cause: Spices not cooked into the sauce.
items.fix: This can happen when spices are added without a sauté step. Stir in a teaspoon of tomato purée and cook on HIGH for 30 minutes uncovered to intensify.

items.cause: Sweet potato released a lot of moisture.
items.fix: Cook uncovered on HIGH for 20 minutes to reduce. A teaspoon of cornflour dissolved in cold water can also thicken it.

content.text: This curry occupies the weekday slot: filling enough for dinner, mild enough for anyone at the table, built from ingredients that are almost always in the house. Serve with basmati rice or warm flatbreads. Leftovers improve overnight; a pot made on Sunday provides Tuesday's lunch.
